perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Blue Hills Parties hereby appoint the Agent as their exclusive financial advisor and conversion agent (i) to utilize its best efforts to solicit subscriptions for Shares and to advise and assist the Holding Company and the Bank with respect to the sale of the Shares in the Offering and (ii) to participate in the Offering in the areas of market making and in syndicate formation or to act as sole book-running manager in the Underwritten Offering (if necessary).
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Blue Hills Parties hereby appoint the Agent as their exclusive financial advisor and conversion agent. The Agent's primary responsibility is to facilitate the administrative aspects of the Conversion. While the Agent may offer suggestions and provide general guidance, the ultimate decision-making authority regarding the solicitation of subscriptions and sale of shares rests solely with the Blue Hills Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by significantly downplaying the Agent's role in soliciting subscriptions and assisting with the sale of Shares. Originally, the Agent was expected to 'utilize its best efforts'. The revised text limits Agent's responsibilities primarily to administrative tasks and shifts the decision making to Blue Hills Parties. This contradicts the original intention where the Agent was expected to be actively involved in sales, therefore creating an In-Text Contradiction if another part of the contract enforces an active role of the agent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All fees or expenses due to the Agent but unpaid will be payable to the Agent in next day funds at the earlier of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date. In the event the Offering is extended beyond the End Date, the Blue Hills Parties and the Agent may agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms. In the event the Holding Company is unable to sell a minimum of 17,850,000 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Holding Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest, as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 7, 9 and 10 hereof. In the event the Offering is terminated for any reason not attributable to the action or inaction of the Agent, the Agent shall be paid the fees due to the date of such termination pursuant to subparagraphs (a) and (e) below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly diminishes the Agent's responsibility from actively soliciting subscriptions and assisting with the sale of Shares to primarily facilitating administrative tasks. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'In the event the Offering is terminated for any reason not attributable to the action or inaction of the Agent, the Agent shall be paid the fees due to the date of such termination pursuant to subparagraphs (a) and (e) below.' If the Agent's role is primarily administrative, it's unclear what 'action or inaction' could lead to the termination of the offering, thus creating an ambiguity regarding fee payment upon termination, and a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All documents made available or delivered by, or to be made available to or delivered by the Blue Hills Parties or their representatives in connection with the issuance and sale of the Shares, including records of account holders and depositors of the Bank, or in connection with the Agent's exercise of due diligence, except for those documents which were prepared by parties other than the Blue Hills Parties or their representatives, were on the dates on which they were delivered, or will be on the dates on which they are to be delivered, true, complete and correct in all material respects.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents made available or delivered by, or to be made available to or delivered by the Blue Hills Parties or their representatives in connection with the issuance and sale of the Shares, including records of account holders and depositors of the Bank, or in connection with the Agent's exercise of due diligence, are believed to be accurate to the best of the Blue Hills Parties' knowledge, but the Blue Hills Parties do not guarantee the completeness or accuracy of these documents. It is the Agent's responsibility to independently verify the information contained therein.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the assurance regarding the accuracy of documents provided by the Blue Hills Parties, shifting the burden of verification to the Agent. Originally, the documents were warranted to be 'true, complete and correct in all material respects.' The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating that the documents are only 'believed to be accurate' and placing the responsibility of due diligence entirely on the agent. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with the sections outlining the reliance of the Agent on information from Blue Hills.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Registration Statement, which was prepared by the Blue Hills Parties and filed with the Commission, has been declared effective by the Commission, no stop order has been issued with respect thereto and no proceedings therefor have been initiated or, to the knowledge of the Blue Hills Parties, threatened by the Commission. At the time the Registration Statement, including the Prospectus contained therein (including any amendment or supplement), became effective, at the Applicable Time 4 (as defined in Section 4(c) hereof) and at the Closing Date, the Registration Statement complied and will comply in all material respects with the requirements of the 1933 Act and the 1933 Act Regulations and the Registration Statement, including the Prospectus contained therein (including any amendment or supplement thereto), and any information regarding the Blue Hills Parties contained in Sales Information (as such term is defined in Section 9 hereof) authorized by the Blue Hills Parties for use in connection with the Offering, did not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, and at the time any Rule 424(b) or (c) Prospectus is filed with the Commission and at the Closing Date referred to in Section 3 hereof, the Prospectus (including any amendment or supplement thereto) and any information regarding the Holding Company contained in Sales Information (as such term is defined in Section 9 hereof) authorized by the Holding Company for use in connection with the Offering contains all statements that are required to be stated therein in accordance with the 1933 Act and the 1933 Act Regulations and does not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; provided, however, that the representations and warranties in this Section 4(a) shall not apply to statements or omissions made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Holding Company by the Agent or its counsel expressly regarding the Agent for use in the Prospectus (the "Agent Information," which the Blue Hills Parties acknowledge appears only in the first sentence of the second paragraph under the caption "The Conversion; Plan of Distribution—Marketing and Distribution; Compensation" in the Prospectus).
perturbation.justification: The original text warrants the accuracy of documents provided. The changed text weakens this warranty and shifts verification to the Agent. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the 'Registration Statement...did not contain an untrue statement of a material fact'. The shift in responsibility and assurance creates a strong in-text contradiction, particularly concerning the Agent's reliance on the Blue Hills Parties' information.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Blue Hills Parties represents and agrees that, unless it obtains the prior consent of the Agent, and the Agent represents and agrees that, unless it obtains the prior consent of the Blue Hills Parties, it has not made and will not make any offer relating to the offered Shares that would constitute an "issuer free writing prospectus" as defined in Rule 433 of the 1933 Act Regulations, or that would constitute a "free writing prospectus," as defined in Rule 405 of the 1933 Act Regulations, required to be filed with the Commission.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Blue Hills Parties represents and agrees that, unless it obtains the prior consent of the Agent, and the Agent represents and agrees that, unless it coordinates with the Blue Hills Parties, it will make any offer relating to the offered Shares that would constitute an "issuer free writing prospectus" as defined in Rule 433 of the 1933 Act Regulations, or that would constitute a "free writing prospectus," as defined in Rule 405 of the 1933 Act Regulations, required to be filed with the Commission.
perturbation.explanation: This change inverts the original intention regarding free writing prospectuses. The original clause prohibited the creation of offers without prior consent. The modified clause mandates creation of offers (free writing prospectus) only by coordination. This contradicts the original text which restricts any extra offers without the prior concent of Blue Hills.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) None of the Blue Hills Parties has directly or indirectly distributed or otherwise used and will not directly or indirectly distribute or otherwise use any prospectus, any "free writing prospectus" (as defined in Rule 405 of the 1933 Act Regulations) or other offering material (including, without limitation, content on the Holding Company's website that may be deemed to be a prospectus, free writing prospectus or other offering material) in connection with the offering and sale of the Shares other than any Permitted Free Writing Prospectus or the Prospectus or other materials permitted by the 1933 Act and the 1933 Act Regulations distributed by the Holding Company and reviewed and approved in advance for distribution by the Agent.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited offers relating to shares without prior consent. The changed text mandates making offers related to shares, as long as they are coordinated, which directly contradicts the intent of the original text. This contradiction is evident when comparing the changed text with the 'Contradicted Text', which explicitly prohibits the distribution or use of any free writing prospectus or other offering material unless it is a 'Permitted Free Writing Prospectus' reviewed and approved in advance. The change introduces a direct conflict regarding the conditions under which such offers can be made, creating a clear in-text contradiction.
